M-060.NAME: mtproto-admin-alerts
M-060.owns: Durable admin notification inbox for MTProto abuse signals, review state, acknowledgements, resolutions, and confirmation-safe enforcement actions.
M-060.purpose: Notify the operator about clear MTProto abuse without automatically blocking users before admin review.

exports.model-MTProtoAdminAlert: Persistent alert row linked to an abuse signal, assignment, user, severity, state, and dedupe key.
exports.fn-create-abuse-alert: Create or update a deduplicated admin alert when abuse severity reaches high or critical thresholds.
exports.fn-list-admin-alerts: Return paginated open/resolved MTProto alerts for the admin panel.
exports.fn-acknowledge-alert: Mark one alert as seen by an admin without changing runtime access.
exports.fn-resolve-alert: Resolve an alert after the admin takes or declines enforcement.
exports.fn-block-ip-for-alert: Create a TTL-bound reviewed IP block and resolve the alert into archive history.

M-060.evidence: pass-local 2026-06-06: alert lifecycle/dedupe/IP-block archive tests, admin analytics API tests, scheduler maintenance regression, frontend-admin build, Phase-43 static smoke, and Phase-79 no-auto-enforcement regression passed.

alert-policy.policy-1: Alert creation is deduplicated by signal type, assignment, severity window, and review state.
alert-policy.policy-2: Alerts do not automatically disable proxies or block IP addresses.
alert-policy.policy-3: Admin enforcement actions require explicit confirmation and write M-026 audit events.
alert-policy.policy-4: Alert details may reference raw IP observation ids, but must not copy raw IP strings into audit logs or general alert payloads.
alert-policy.policy-5: Reviewed enforcement actions resolve alerts out of the open inbox while preserving history for archive review.
alert-policy.policy-6: MTProto admin alerts are advisory until the operator explicitly confirms disable-proxy or TTL IP-block actions.

constraints.constraint-1: Proxy disable is safer than IP blocking and should remain the primary enforcement action.
constraints.constraint-2: IP block actions require TTL, double confirmation, and verification that the address came from M-061 client IP evidence.
constraints.constraint-3: Mobile-network NAT and carrier-grade NAT can make IP blocking overbroad; the UI must make this risk visible to the operator.

phase-79-executed-scope.scope: Made the no-auto-enforcement contract executable in tests: alert creation never changes assignment status, runtime policy, or IP block state until an explicit admin-confirmed action route is called.
phase-79-executed-scope.verification: PASS local: backend MTProto admin-alert tests and Phase-79 smoke prove alert creation/review does not auto-disable proxies or auto-block IPs.

M-060.hotspot: H-047: MTProto abuse alerts must notify admins without automatically punishing users before operator review
